long_description:
Multiple patches of updating ACL rules is working on few endpoints and not working for few endpoints. This is impacting CBR policies for customers
This is a continuation of earlier INC10489523 (CSE) / INC10489575 (IKS)
Changes to CBR rules for Kubernetes Service cluster masters might not take effect
Incident appears to be intermittent, but not hard to recreate
short_description: Update of ACL rules is showing intermittent failures
close_notes:
All the regions have been completed with seagent restart and IKS team has been asked to run the tests and come back if any issues observed.
Considering the SPP in place and teams continuously working to get a permanent stability.
current_status_next_steps:
Issue is mitigated.
All the regions have been completed with seagent restart and IKS team has been asked to run the tests and come back if any issues observed.
Considering the SPP in place , teams continuously working to get a permanent stability.
customer_facing_impact:
- Changes to CBR rules for Kubernetes Service cluster masters might not take effect, allowing either traffic that should be blocked under the updated CBR rules to get through, or not allowing traffic that should be allowed. This could result in a security issue for customers.
- No other cluster functionality is affected.

Times in UTC
[2025-DEC-06]
13:42 AAA Impact Start
05:28 (DEC-07) AAA Sev4 CIE Confirmed
18:45 AAA Ian Jack Joining as AVM OnDuty
18:51 IKS It would appear that the problem from earlier (INC10489575) was never completely fixed, The most recent test failure  was at Sat Dec  6 13:42:01 UTC 2025, so taking that as the start time.
19:12 AVM paged CSE to join teams call
19:24 IKS reports that problem is intermittent, but not hard to recreate. Around 50% of requests result in failure
19:29 IKS is phoning CSE oncall SRE (no response and no Voicemail
19:46 AVM contacted CSE oncall who is unable to assist and suggested to contact May Lambert. Left voicemail to contact  AVM
19:51 IKS escalated to Shaival Chokshi who is going to make some calls.
20:01 AVM escalated to sev3. This is a security exposure for customers if not resolved.
20:05 CSE engineer engaged and investigating
20:41 CSE confirmed that this appears to be similar to INC10477204 from Thursday 4th Dec
20:48 CSE re-triggered PATCH in SE 166.9.34.50 and ACL's seems to be applied for the eu-gb endpoint that IKS recreated on
20:52 IKS paging out their network team to test if this is resolved
20:52 CSE are asking IKS what other endpoints/CSE VIP's have this issue
21:30 CSE need to look further to understand the issue so are creating a script to collect data. This may take some time, so they will reach out to IKS when they understand why this keeps happening. The teams call has been closed for the time being.
22:23 CSE Believe ETCD database service is giving empty response without giving error even though data present in ETCD.
22:32 CSE engaging etcd support team
23:33 CSE team are still analysing the data to identify what has happened 

[2025-DEC-07]
01:15  IKS report that they have 2 IKS cluster issues (eu-gb region) reported from the wxd SRE team team as well as the CodeEngine team's clusters.
cluster lh-icd-prod-eu-gb-db-ekgwj, cluster id: cle3a4nl02ifri83sam0, region: eu-gu, HealthStatus: All Workers Critical
cluster lh-icd-prod-eu-gb-db-wqntb, cluster id: d20aq1bl0au6mv7lcpqg, region: eu-gu, HealthStatus: All Workers Critical
01:33 CSE After deleting that IP from ACL list, I do see the NAT table rule without any ipset binding
01:34 IKS script shows that those master ports are now accessible.  So think within the next 5 minutes those workers should starting going to Ready status
01:51 SRE, I see at least all the worker status are normal on their 2 clusters. Currently checking with the CodeEngine team now to see if their issue is gone.
01:58 SRE, 2 IKS cluster (eu-gb region),  reported from the wxd SRE team, are back to HealthStatus: All Workers Normal.
02:10 IKS, We had another test fail.  We added these ACLs to a different set of CSEs, but our test was still able to access port 30772 on at least one of the IPs in the multi-zone CSE even though the CSE ACLs should have blocked it. Shared  IPs for those CSEs with CSE team
02:30 Team is trying to isolate the specific CSE IPs
02:55 AVM checks with CSE if they had any findings
02:57 CSE, We are currently debugging further
03:33 AVM checks for status from CSE MGR
03:35 CSE Though team is debugging this further to get to the cause, I believe there is no ongoing issue at the moment. Please confirm the same
03:49 Iks still seeing CSE ACL problems on some of the CSEs that we have looked at.I was able to recreate with a cluster on a different set of CSEs as well, so it isn't an isolated incident.
04:03 IKS, you applied some fix to ALL CSEs last night.  But these problems are showing up today.  So whatever fix you made last night/this morning either didn't fix the problem, or only fixed it for a few hours
04:03 CSE We are trying to reproduce the issue in our local environment for us to be able to debug this.
04:04 IKS, and again, this isn't some problem that only shows up on one or two CSEs.  Roughly half of the tests I run hit a problem like this.  So just "fixing" the few that I point out that I find by running tests isn't going to fix the overall problem.
I agree, it is probably necessary for you to recreate this in your environment, unless you can get to the root cause from the few test failures I've reported
04:05 CSE We are trying to reproduce the issue in our local environment for us to be able to debug this.
05:28 CSE created their own CIE
10:22 CSE have been unable to recreate, so have requested assistance from IKS. AVM are contacting
10:37 IKS oncall is looking to page out IKS network team
11:18 IKS oncall have paged out Brad
11:42 CSE have created a binary to provide additional logging and are asking for a sample endpoint to apply this to and recreate to collect additional diags 
11:50 IKS have provided 4 endpoints that can be used for testing
12:08 CSE have applied the binary to us-south-stage-us-south-tugboat105-se-private-01-iks-allcustomers-dedi-st-1g-6fdddfd1 and IKS are starting the recreate.
12:15 IKS recreated, but it landed on an endpoint that had not been patched. CSE will patch some more end points before we try again.
12:45 CSE have patched all 8 endpoints supplied by IKS
13:02 IKS are unable to create on the staging endpoints that have been patched. However there is no adverse effect, so if we need to apply the patch to prod then there should not be any issue
13:37 CSE advise that the restarts that were actioned on 6th would have been a fresh load of ACL from ETCD, so at that point all ACL will have been correctly implemented. It will only be any ACL updates since then that might be compromised.
13:40 CSE are suggesting a mitigation to manually monitor for ACL updates and check to see if they have been correctly implemented before manually correcting if needed. This would buy time to fully diagnose the root cause.
14:15 IKS have requested 2 further endpoints be patched which will help their recreation plan
14:18 AVM has alerted IKS execs of the current status and action plan
14:54 CSE have patched the additional endpoints
16:25 IKS have successfully recreated on a patched end point. CSE are analysing the new logs
18:29 CSE have confirmed that ETCD read is giving us inconsistent data while reading. from 2 different VM's. Same happens when we do read the same data from our Bastion.
18:30 ETCD support are assisting
19:45 CSE team are looking for workarounds for if etcd cant resolve. They are considering bringing forward a plan to migrate off etcd, which was scheduled for Q1
21:30 CSE noted inconsistency between the ETCD db master and replica nodes.  The CSE's plan calls for one of the ETCD replica nodes to be recreated from the Postgress db, which is the source of truth for CSEs; thus no risk of loosing data.  Once the replica is created, one of the replica nodes will be replaced.  The plan will commence at 22:00 UTC upon databases SMEs shift change.
23:20 CSE's plan to recreate one of the ETCD replica nodes from the Postgress db and replace one of the replica nodes will not work as etcd is end of life and provisioning a new node is not allowed. We are awating further advice from ETCD
23:37 Team are trying to page out etcd technical resource

[2025-DEC-08]
00:30: Kara is trying to engage EU manager as India team is not sure about ETCD.
00:37: EU manager has been looped to get a resource to join and  to assist.
1:00: Udo Bussmann joined the call.
01:05 Per the discussion AVM Mgr advised to upgrade the CIE to sev 2
1:22: CSE : There is no suspicion defrag command cast is having inconsistency
1;30 the database exceeded 8GB, which was unsupported and led to read/write failures. The SCD team recommended a defragmentation, which was performed. Afterward, the team ran an internal sync-up agent to resynchronize with the master PostgreSQL database. However, inconsistencies in read results appeared the following day. The current working theory is that fragmentation contributed to these issues.
01:34 The team demonstrated an inconsistency between expected ETCD behavior and what the system is returning through the data-path code. Although a port value was previously added and then deleted—confirmed by direct ETCD queries—the system's code continues to read the port as if it still exists. This mismatch suggests that stale or inconsistent ETCD data is being returned, which may be causing incorrect traffic handling or blocking behavior related to CBR rules.
01:50 The team noted unusual size imbalance across ETCD nodes, with one node significantly larger than the others. While uneven sizes can be normal, this discrepancy may be due to un-defragmented space. The database had previously reached the 8GB limit before being defragged. The team is considering another defrag, mainly on the oversized node, to reclaim space and restore balance.
1:57: Based on discussion it is found that DB backup is already completed,  defrag all the databases nodes and see.
2:11: DB backup is started and defrag will be done post backup.
2:40: Defrag is completed for M1. validation in progress. Proposed to see defrag can be done on M0 and M2.Cost changes is done and minimized in M1 as read is successful as data is there on time.
2:51: Defrag is done on all DB nodes. Still seeing inconsistently wrong result from read from CD.
3:25: another proposal to restart SC agents across all the systems to see whether improvement can be seen
03:57 SC agent restart on a single deice has been done and issue on the specific agent is now resolved.
03:58 New connections work but the old connections still showing issue. Team is continuing to identify possible remediation plan
04:27 CSE team has planned to perform seagents restart on all the regions as a workaround , which can help achieve the CIE resolution. As a broader scope Cos migration will be considered outside the CIE window.
04:42 09 out of 12 regions are now completed with seagents restart
04:50 11 out of 12 regions are now completed
04:51 The team acknowledged that while the issue may recur, restarting the SE agents provides a temporary mitigation and restores consistent ETCD reads based on current test results. This should allow CIES validation to proceed and enable IKS testing to run successfully for at least several hours. The temporary fix gives the team time to continue developing long-term solutions—such as migrating to COS or implementing additional mitigation strategies.Given the intermittent nature of the problem; the team recommends proceeding with SE agent restarts across all regions and revisiting the issue if it resurfaces.IKS team continue to run tests on their env. If this issue reoccurs we will re-engage. At this time ICD team is on the standby/monitoring mode.
05:12 All the regions have been completed with seagent restart and IKS team has been asked to run the tests and come back if any issues observed.call has been closed
05:13 CIE can be resolved, considering the SPP in place and teams continuously working to get a permanent stability.

=============================
               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================
Service Protection Plan:

Long term plan by CSE
OS migration process involving four steps: (1) create a new COS bucket, (2) sync all configuration data from the control plane (Postgres) to this bucket, (3) update the control plane to write to both COS and ETCD temporarily, and (4) update the SE agents in the target region—(one regions at a time) to read from COS instead of ETCD. Other regions can continue using ETCD until they are migrated.

By ETCD
They may need to look at the option of reconstructing the cluster if the issue re-occurs, (later in the day). 



Actions taken by ETCD:
Defrag was done. We had previously discussed and addressed their concern about doing the defrag, which was that the member nodes would show different size. Niall had confirmed this to be normal mode of operation for ETCD.  The size got smaller on all nodes.
When using the ETCD client dirctly they could not reproduce the problem.
In their code, they could however, using an existing old connection, consistently see one case where a wrong value was returned
They restarted their code, and currently cannot see the issue.
@NiallF has done handover to @PAWAN SHARMA they went through the history and assessed all available runbooks again. In particular it is documented how to drop and recreate a pod, which may be required if the problem re-occurs.
